items.title: Crypto users targeted in ‘elaborate’ scam using popular notes app
items.summary: Elastic Security Labs says a multi-step social engineering scam is aimed at those in crypto and finance, using a community plugin feature on a note-taking app to spread malicious device-controlling software.

items.title: Bitcoiners propose freezing quantum-vulnerable coins in BIP-361
items.summary: The authors described it as a “private incentive to upgrade” because lost or frozen coins will make everyone else’s coins worth slightly more.

items.title: Mutation testing for the agentic era
items.summary: Code coverage is one of the most dangerous quality metrics in software testing. Many developers fail to realize that code coverage lies by omission: it measures execution, not verification. Test suites with high coverage can obfuscate the fact that critical functionality is untes

items.title: Try our new dimensional analysis Claude plugin
items.summary: We’re releasing a new Claude plugin for developing and auditing code that implements dimensional analysis, a technique we explored in our most recent blog post. Most LLM-based security skills ask the model to find bugs. Our new dimensional-analysis plugin for Claude Code takes a

items.title: Spotting issues in DeFi with dimensional analysis
items.summary: Using dimensional analysis, you can categorically rule out a whole category of logic and arithmetic bugs that plague DeFi formulas. No code changes required, just better reasoning! One of the first lessons in physics is learning to think in terms of dimensions. Physicists can oft

items.title: Crypto, banks continue Senate bill spat with new proposal concerns: Report
items.summary: Senator Thom Tillis will publicly share an agreement to end a crypto and banking clash over stablecoin yields, but both sides are resisting the proposal, Politico reports.

items.title: The next Fed chair has crypto exposure in his portfolio. Here are the details
items.summary: Kevin Warsh's financial disclosure reveals stakes in DeFi protocols, Ethereum scaling networks, a Bitcoin Lightning startup, and prediction markets — all of which he's promised to sell.

items.title: Ethereum Foundation unveils $1M audit subsidy program to boost crypto security and cut costs for builders
items.summary: The new initiative aims to address a persistent challenge in crypto development—the high cost of smart contract security audits.

items.title: Bitcoin climbs to highest level since Feb. 5 crash that sent price plunging to $60,000
items.summary: Optimism over developments in the Middle East sparked a sharp decline in oil prices and rallies across risk markets.

items.title: JPMorgan CFO warns stablecoins risk becoming ‘regulatory arbitrage’ play
items.summary: During the bank's earnings call on Tuesday, JPMorgan CFO Jeremy Barnum warned that stablecoins could become a tool for regulatory arbitrage unless they are held to the same strict oversight and consumer protection standards as traditional bank deposits.

items.title: Federal Reserve chair nominee's disclosure includes crypto and AI holdings
items.summary: Kevin Warsh did not include the value of several crypto and AI investments among his disclosures as he awaits his confirmation hearing before the Senate Banking Committee.

items.title: Ethereum treasury firm Bitmine reports $3.8 billion Q1 loss in latest filing
items.summary: The largest corporate ether holder posted a $3.8 billion quarterly loss as its pivot from mining to ETH accumulation continues.

items.title: Bitcoin developers are trying to build quantum defenses. Your coins could pay the price.
items.summary: A proposal has been updated on Bitcoin's official repository, calling for freezing of quantum-vulnerable coins.

items.title: XRP climbs toward $1.38 as Japan's Rakuten integrates token for payments
items.summary: Breakout backed by institutional flows and whale buying, with Japan adoption adding a fresh demand narrative.

items.title: Ether-bitcoin ratio bounces from 2026 lows, signaling broader crypto recovery
items.summary: The ETH/BTC ratio hit its highest since January as Ethereum's network added 284,000 new users in Q1 and stablecoin supply reached a record $180 billion.

items.title: SOL, ADA, DOGE pullback, bitcoin holds above $74,000 as Asia recoups Iran war losses
items.summary: China's CSI 300 joins Taiwan and Singapore in erasing war-related declines as US-Iran talk optimism keeps oil below $100; spot ETFs posted $471 million in single-day inflows last week.

items.title: North Korean hackers used AI-enabled social engineering in Zerion attack
items.summary: It is the second long-term social-engineering attack this month, after the $280 million exploit of the Drift Protocol.

items.title: X rolls out smart cashtags in US, Canada in step toward ‘everything app’
items.summary: Tat Thang, a partner at the prediction platform Polymarket, suggests X is building a Web3 equivalent of the Chinese WeChat Pay citing its recent moves to scrub crypto bots.

items.title: Apple removes fake Ledger app that stole $9.5M from crypto investors
items.summary: Apple told Cointelegraph that the fake Ledger Live app was removed and that the developer was terminated from its app store.

items.title: Switzerland’s Crypto Valley funding rose 37% in 2025 as TON led deals
items.summary: Crypto Valley raised $728 million across 31 deals in 2025, outpacing global blockchain funding growth as a $400 million TON deal lifted totals.
